text.title: Research Repo File-Naming Convention Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Research Repo File-Naming Convention application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Filenames MUST be lowercase kebab-case slugs with no spaces, accents, or PII.
rule.rationale: Spaces and accents break grep, S3 sync, and most LLM-tools.

rule.statement: Every artefact filename MUST start with ISO date prefix YYYY-MM-DD.
rule.rationale: Date-prefix makes sort-by-name produce chronological order.

rule.statement: Participant ids MUST be Pnnn (P001..P999); real names are forbidden in filenames.
rule.rationale: Real names in filenames leak PII into backups, search indexes, and LLM contexts.

rule.statement: Every artefact MUST carry a study-type tag from the closed set {interview, usability, survey, diary, intercept}.
rule.rationale: Open-set tags break the synthesis manifest.

rule.statement: Every research repo MUST carry a manifest.json regenerated on each commit.
rule.rationale: Without manifest, LLM agents cannot navigate without reading every body.
